display_name: General Affidavit (Sworn/Affirmed Before Notary Public or Oath Commissioner)
structural_summary: A general-purpose affidavit sworn or affirmed before a Notary Public or Oath Commissioner. Layout: stamp-paper guidance line; centred title AFFIDAVIT; deponent introduction (name, parentage, age, occupation, residential address); solemn affirmation opening ("I, ..., do hereby solemnly affirm and declare as under"); numbered declarations each beginning "That", reciting the operative facts (name variants, residence, study gap, lost document, income, or date of birth depending on purpose); a purpose paragraph naming the authority before which the affidavit will be produced; verification clause with place and date affirming the contents are true and correct to the deponent's knowledge and belief; deponent signature block (DEPONENT); notarial attestation block ("Solemnly affirmed before me") with seal. No statutory citations appear in the body.
statutory_requirements:
- Recite the deponent's full name, parentage, age, occupation and residential address in the opening.
- Open with the secular affirmation: "do hereby solemnly affirm and declare as under".
- Number every substantive declaration; conventionally each paragraph begins "That".
- Include a purpose paragraph stating why the affidavit is sworn and the authority before which it will be produced.
- End with a verification clause recording place and date and affirming the contents are true and correct to the deponent's knowledge and belief.
- Provide a DEPONENT signature block and a notarial attestation block ("Solemnly affirmed before me") with the notary's seal and register particulars [VERIFY: attestation/register formalities under the Notaries Rules, 1956].
- Statutory citations are NOT customary in the body of a general affidavit; the declarations should cite no Acts or sections.
- Execute on non-judicial stamp paper; the value varies by state and purpose, so carry placeholder guidance such as "[TO BE EXECUTED ON NON-JUDICIAL STAMP PAPER OF ₹ [VALUE] AS APPLICABLE IN THE STATE]" instead of a fixed amount [VERIFY: applicable State Stamp Act schedule entry for affidavits].
- The notary's power to take affidavits flows from the Notaries Act, 1952; affirmations are governed by the Oaths Act, 1969 — cite neither in the body.
- A false affidavit may attract prosecution for giving false evidence [VERIFY: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 provisions replacing IPC ss. 191/193]; any cautionary recital should omit section numbers.

fields.name: deponent_parent_name
fields.description: Parentage recital (son of / daughter of / wife of)

fields.name: deponent_age
fields.description: Stated as 'aged about N years' in the opening recital

fields.name: city
fields.description: City of residence and of verification/swearing

fields.name: deponent_id_number
fields.description: Optional identity recital in the introduction

fields.name: name_variant_a
fields.description: First spelling/form of the name, with the document it appears in

fields.name: name_variant_b
fields.description: Second spelling/form; affidavit affirms both denote one and the same person

fields.name: residing_since_year
fields.placeholder: [YEAR FROM WHICH DEPONENT RESIDES AT THE ADDRESS]

fields.name: gap_start_year
fields.placeholder: [ACADEMIC YEAR IN WHICH THE STUDY GAP BEGAN]

fields.name: gap_reason
choices: deponent's own prolonged illness and recovery
caring for an ailing family member

fields.name: date_of_birth
fields.description: Deponent's correct date of birth as affirmed; 25-60 years in the past
